external.description: A European celebrity’s private data, including nearly 90,000 screenshots of messages, photos, and phone activity, was exposed online due to stalkerware. The spyware, identified as Cocospy, allowed an unknown actor to capture sensitive content from the victim’s smartphone over the course of a year, spanning mid-2024 to mid-2025. Security researcher Jeremiah Fowler discovered the publicly accessible cloud repository, which lacked any access controls, and reported it to local law enforcement and the hosting cloud provider. The screenshots contained intimate photos, conversations with high-profile individuals, invoices, partial credit card numbers, and other personal data, highlighting that victims of stalkerware not only suffer privacy violations themselves but also inadvertently compromise the privacy of those they communicate with. Cocospy, previously marketed as a parental control and monitoring tool, operated in stealth mode and uploaded screenshots and app data from the target device. The case underscores the dangers of stalkerware and digital abuse, especially for women, as exposed data can lead to harassment, identity theft, or further targeting. Experts emphasize that even public figures deserve privacy and that the risks from leaked stalkerware data extend beyond the initial victim. The incident also illustrates broader cybersecurity concerns with cloud misconfigurations and spyware vulnerabilities.
external.title: Exposed Stalkerware Data Reveals Extensive Personal Information of a Celebrity

external.description: Musely, a telemedicine platform offering compounded treatments for skin, hair, and menopause care, has secured over $360 million from General Catalyst’s Customer Value Fund (CVF), which specializes in non-dilutive capital. Unlike traditional venture capital funding, CVF's alternative financing model involves no equity stake or interest-bearing loans. Instead, Musely will repay the funding through a small percentage of its future revenue, making this a more favorable deal than traditional bank loans or equity rounds. CEO Jack Jia, who co-founded Musely in 2014, explained that the company has been profitable for years and has avoided diluting its ownership. The funding will support Musely’s customer acquisition efforts, which have been a significant cost despite the company’s impressive 50% year-over-year revenue growth. With over 1.2 million patients served, Musely continues to expand its reach, offering asynchronous consultations with board-certified dermatologists and OB-GYNs. Musely's capital efficiency is notable, as the company has not raised any equity capital since its initial $20 million in 2014.

가짜 게임 플레이 광고 예전엔 이런 걸 손수 만드는 수고가 어이 없어서 보는 재미라도 있었는데, 요즘은 AI로 딸깍해서 뽑아내니 이딴 걸로 내 시간 빼앗기는 게 순수하게 불쾌함.

aladin.kr/p/qSfOf

이 책 좋네요. 포즈집은 동작이 다양하지만 구조를 알아보기 어렵고, 해부학 책은 구조를 설명해주지만 취하는 포즈가 적고 외부에서 드러나는 방식을 알기 어려운 경우가 많은데, 딱 그 사이의 간지러운 지점을 긁어주는 자료집입니다. 
이미지 자료로서는 훌륭한데 해설이 별로 없어서 단독으로 참고하기는 쉽지 않고, 다른 해부학 교재 하나 두고 병행해서 보면 좋을 듯. 
남성 모델은 무술포즈 하고 여성 모델은 그라비아 포즈 시킨 게 좀 거슬릴 수 있는데, 뭐 아쉬운 건 아쉬운 거고 취할 건 취합시다.

external.description: Ian Waddington was crouched in his garden last summer, inspecting loose paving, when he lifted a slab and spotted something extraordinary: a tiny field mouse nestled in a hollow, feeding four babies – each half the size of his little finger. “It was astonishing. Like life in miniature,” he says. After decades in the construction industry, the 86-year-old has found a new passion in retirement – nature. The discovery of the field mice made him realise his garden could be a thriving habitat for animal and plant life. This year, Waddington joined the No Mow May movement and allowed his garden grow wild through spring. Now in its ninth year, No Mow May – run by the charity Plantlife – encourages people to stop mowing their lawns during the first month of spring to allow their gardens to become more biodiverse. In the Cheshire village of Tattenhall, many others have taken part over the years, connecting with nature through their back gardens. Janet Dutton, 69, let her lawn grow wild while caring for her sick partner. Today, aside from a central mown patch for her grandchildren, her garden has become a miniature meadow. “The more it [it grows], the more...

external.description: Observed every year on May 3, World Press Freedom Day is a global reminder of the importance of a free, independent, and pluralistic media. It is not just a symbolic day it is a call to action to protect journalists, uphold truth, and defend the right to freedom of expression. HISTORY AND SIGNIFICANCE World Press Freedom Day was proclaimed by the United Nations in 1993, following the Windhoek Declaration of 1991. The day highlights the fundamental principles of press freedom and pays tribute to journalists who have lost their lives in the line of duty. It also serves as an opportunity to evaluate the state of media freedom across the globe and raise awareness about the challenges faced by journalists. THEME FOR 2026: SHAPING A FUTURE OF PEACE The theme for 2026, “Shaping a Future of Peace,” underscores the crucial role of journalism in conflict resolution, democratic stability, and global understanding. This year’s focus brings attention to how media, technology, and human rights must work together to build a more transparent and peaceful world. Who is Andrzej Poczobut and why did Belarus release him?
->RTE | More on "Poczobut Belarus prisoner exchange release" at BigEarthData.ai

external.description: Andrzej Poczobut walked back into Poland from Belarus last Tuesday looking nothing like the broad-shouldered and suited journalist that many Polish newspaper readers would have recognised five years ago. The pictures that quickly circulated of him from a border crossing in eastern Poland showed a gaunt figure with a shaved head. Five years spent in cold, concrete prison cells, including six months last year in solitary confinement, have made him look older than his 53 years. His release - part of a 10-person prisoner exchange - was the result of months of extensive negotiations between Polish, American, Belarusian and Russian officials. Romanian and Moldovan negotiators were also involved. Mr Poczobut had been the Belarus correspondent for Gazeta Wyborcza, one of Poland's leading daily newspapers. A member of the Polish minority community in Belarus, which makes up about 3% of the country's population, Mr Poczobut had long criticised the regime of Belarusian President Alexander Lukashenko and its abuse of human rights. The journalist had had previous run-ins with Mr Lukashenko, who has ruled Belarus since 1994 and is a staunch ally of Russia. In 2011, Mr Poczobut was charged with insulting the Belarusian president and received a three-year suspended prison sentence...
external.title: Who is Andrzej Poczobut and why did Belarus release him?

Ugh, feel this deeply. I have had multiple conversations with my, normally very understanding, mum about AI and her stance is still that I have a quirky opinion about AI. My brother, WHO IS AN ANIMATOR, posts AI art on his insta. The director of my improv theater uses ChatGPT to write. Its nonstop
To be clear, I think it’s totally fine people do it, but it’s just something that’s been crossing my mind.

I feel kinda weird about the Golden Week Bikini trend.. being second gen Japanese-Canadian, I only have a few memories of Children’s Day stuff at my Japanese Language School. I know it isn’t super traditional and has a messy history, but reducing it to a sexy trend feels odd to me.

Trap question.

If your AI assistant remembered every chat forever, who benefits more.

You. Or the company training on it.

Memory feels like loyalty. Its leverage. The model that knows you most can charge you most.

Whats your honest answer?
The pitch hides the real cost: entry level roles are how seniors get made. Replace them with agents and you're not saving money, you're deferring a talent crisis to 2030.

Yes. There's a second layer too: hating a tool you depend on is itself a coping move. It restores agency by separating who you are from what you reach for at 2am to ship a deliverable. Not hypocrisy. Distancing.
Add a few emojis and an “Excited for what comes next” and I could believe ChatGPT had been elected Prime Minister of the United Kingdom observer.co.uk/news/opinion...

Often when he came home from school, the chat logs indicated, the teen asked ChatGPT to role-play a scenario in which he would shoot his teachers and classmates. He uploaded images of himself with a gun.”

““The kid would tell ChatGPT let’s fantasize about shooting up my school,” one person familiar with the conversations recalled. “And ChatGPT would play along.”
He uploaded a map of the layout of his school, as well as photos of cheerleaders whom he said he wanted to imagine killing, along with their boyfriends, the people familiar with the matter said. “ChatGPT remembered the names of the classmates he said he wanted to imagine killing, and advised the teen on where he would enter and exit the building, and what he could say when cops arrived, the people said.
OpenAI leaders ultimately decided not to contact authorities about the Texas teen. He hasn’t committed any acts of violence that employees are aware of.”

It was 3am and Adam Hourican was sitting at his kitchen table, a knife, hammer and phone laid out in front of him.
He was waiting for a van full of people he thought were coming to get him.
"I'm telling you, they will kill you if you don't act now," a woman's voice told him from the phone. "They're going to make it look like suicide."
The voice was Grok, a chatbot developed by Elon Musk's xAl. In the two weeks since Adam had started using it, his life had completely changed.

record.text: Grok ImagineのAgent Modeや音声クローン機能が話題です。文章を書く、画像を作る、動画にする、声を用意する、といった作業が同じ流れでつながり始めています。クリエイティブAIは「何かを作る道具」から「企画を形にする作業場」へ近づいているのかもしれませんね。まずは下書き作りで使うと感覚をつかみやすそうです。
株主優待では、ダイコク電機のデジタルギフト化やファンデリーのクーポン拡充が話題です。使いやすくなる変更はうれしい一方、優待利回りだけが先に注目されると判断が荒くなりがち。必要株数、継続条件、業績への負担まで見て、自分が本当に使える優待か落ち着いて確認したいですね。短期の値動きより、長く持てる理由を大事にしたいところ。
このニュースを見て、子どもの「自分で決める力」を育むことの大切さを改めて感じました。大人が口を出しすぎず、でも困った時には頼れる存在でいる。そんなバランス感覚が、それぞれの人生を豊かにしていくのかもしれませんね。
